rationale: The proposed GenAI chatbot can help alleviate the growing ticket volume and reduce average response time, but its implementation must be carefully managed to address compliance risks, people impact, and governance gaps.
financial_considerations: Projected payback period: 9-12 months, based on a 35% reduction in ticket load and associated cost savings
operational_considerations: The chatbot can answer FAQs, summarize customer issues, and create draft responses for agents, potentially reducing the workload of human agents
The chatbot may not be able to handle complex customer issues, requiring human intervention and oversight
people_impact: Potential job losses among human agents, although the chatbot may also enable agents to focus on more complex and high-value tasks
Need for training and upskilling of agents to work effectively with the chatbot
compliance_risks: Personal information in tickets may be accessed or processed by the chatbot, requiring robust data protection measures
Need for compliance with relevant data protection regulations, such as GDPR or CCPA
conditions_for_approval: Implementation of robust data protection measures to safeguard personal information in tickets
Development and implementation of AI governance policies to ensure accountability, transparency, and fairness in the use of the chatbot
Regular review and assessment of the chatbot's performance and impact on customer support operations
Provision of training and upskilling opportunities for human agents to work effectively with the chatbot
final_recommendation: Approve the deployment of the GenAI chatbot for first-level customer support, subject to the conditions outlined above, and closely monitor its implementation and impact on customer support operations.
